input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | Compilers, headers |
| **.NET SDK (latest)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | .NET runtime + CLI |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically by MAUI workload, or [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) | Android build toolchain |

---

## 2. Installation Steps

```bash
# 1. Install Xcode from the App Store, then accept the license:
sudo xcodebuild -license accept

# 2. Install .NET SDK (use the macOS Arm64 or x64 installer from dot.net)
#    Verify:
dotnet --version

# 3. Install the MAUI workload:
sudo dotnet workload install maui

# 4. The Android SDK, NDK, and emulator images are pulled automatically
#    by the maui workload. To confirm installed workloads:
dotnet workload list
```

---

## 3. Discover the Right Versions

- **Supported .NET version**: Check [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/what-is-maui](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/what-is-maui) — MAUI ships with the latest .NET (currently .NET 8+).
- **Xcode version required**: See [learn.microsoft.com/xamarin/ios/release-notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/ios/apple-account) or run `dotnet workload update` which will tell you if your Xcode is too old.
- **Android API levels**: The workload installs the recommended SDK levels. Customize via `dotnet android sdk list` or Android SDK Manager.

---

## 4. Verify the Setup

```bash
# Check all dependencies in one shot:
dotnet maui check

# Create and run a test project:
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# iOS (requires Xcode simulator):
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-ios

# Android (requires emulator or device):
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-android
```

`dotnet maui check` will report any missing components (Xcode version, Android SDK, JDK, etc.) and suggest fixes.

---

## 5. Optional but Recommended

- **Visual Studio Code** + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) — lightweight IDE with Hot Reload support.
- **Android Emulator**: Create a device via `avdmanager` or VS Code's device picker.
- **Apple Developer Account**: Required for deploying to physical iOS devices (free account works for debugging).

---

**Key official docs**: [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation)
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete setup plan for developing .NET MAUI apps on macOS targeting iOS and Android. They can't run commands right now, so they need a reference guide. Let me provide a comprehensive answer based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI development setup on macOS.
